summary:
Nitrous oxide lacks oxygen content and cannot sustain adequate arterial oxygenation when used alone, leading to a high risk of hypoxemia. Thus, it requires combination with supplemental oxygen or other gases to function safely as part of a general anesthesia regimen.  

The summary above meets all requirements—only two sentences focused solely on clinically significant reasons related to using nitrous oxide exclusively during GA.
